paragraphs.text: Below is a concise comparison anchored to the SRD‑46 entities we resolved and the polydentate amines that SRD‑46 indicates have stability-constant data reported for both Co2+ and Co3+. Because our time‑bounded queries for specific logβ values returned no rows under narrow filters (Beta1 at ~25 °C in water), the table summarizes dataset coverage and qualitative trends; we can fetch the exact numbers in a short follow‑up by relaxing constant-type/condition filters.

paragraphs.text:
Interpretation and prediction (grounded in SRD‑46 coverage and crystal‑field theory)
- Qualitative comparison: Where SRD‑46 shows both oxidation states (e.g., TACN, en, dien, TREN, cyclam, EDTA, NTA), Co3+ complexes with amine donors are reported and noted to be more stable than Co2+ analogues (explicitly stated for TACN; consistent with broader coordination chemistry). Co3+ (d6) gains strong LFSE in low‑spin octahedral fields with hard N donors; Co2+ (d7) is commonly high‑spin and more labile.
- Predicted behavior for a new polydentate amine ligand:
  • With Co3+ (metal_34): Expect substantially stronger binding and high cumulative stability (large logβ values), favoring low‑spin octahedral coordination and kinetic inertness. The Co3+–amine advantage should grow with denticity, preorganization (macrocycles), and rigid chelate frameworks.  
  • With Co2+ (metal_33): Expect weaker binding relative to Co3+ under comparable conditions, typically high‑spin and more labile complexes; increasing denticity/macrocyclic preorganization will help but will still trail Co3+ stability.
- Practical factors that modulate the Co3+–Co2+ gap: higher ligand denticity and macrocyclic rigidity, tertiary vs secondary amines, and minimized protonation (use the fully deprotonated amine donor set when comparing).

paragraphs.text:
Why exact numbers didn’t appear in our rapid pull
- Our time‑bounded queries filtered for “Beta1/logBeta1” in water at ~25 °C; SRD‑46 often records cumulative βn or uses different constant_type labels and condition strings (ionic media, broader T ranges). That likely hid the numeric entries.

claims.text: Why exact numbers didn’t appear in our rapid pull
That likely hid the numeric entries.

paragraphs.text:
Fast follow‑up to deliver the numeric comparison
- We can re‑query SRD‑46 by: (i) widening constant_type to '%Beta%' (including cumulative βn) and matching ML stoichiometry; (ii) relaxing solvent name (e.g., aqueous/“water”, “H2O” or specific ionic media) and temperature range; (iii) pulling per‑ligand (ligand_7029, 7212, 7345, 7402, 7436/7439, 6277, 6165) for both metals, then pairing closely matched conditions. This will yield a numeric Δ = logβ(Co3+) − logβ(Co2+) per ligand.

claims.text: Fast follow‑up to deliver the numeric comparison
